question: What is the difference between an Oracle INSTANCE and a DATABASE?
options: The INSTANCE is the memory (SGA) plus background processes; the DATABASE is the set of files on disk (datafiles, control files, redo logs). An instance mounts and opens a database
The database is the memory; the instance is on disk
An instance is a single table

explanation.uz: Instance — xotira (SGA) + background jarayonlar (o'tkinchi, o'chirsa yo'qoladi). Database — diskdagi fayllar (datafile, control file, redo log). Instance database'ni mount qilib, ochadi. (RAC'da bitta database'ga bir nechta instance bo'ladi.)
explanation.en: The instance is memory (SGA) + background processes (transient, gone when shut down). The database is the files on disk (datafiles, control files, redo logs). An instance mounts and opens a database. (In RAC, several instances serve one database.)

question: Which tablespace holds the data dictionary and core system metadata?
options: SYSTEM (with SYSAUX as its auxiliary companion for extra metadata)

explanation.uz: SYSTEM tablespace ma'lumotlar lug'ati (data dictionary) va asosiy tizim obyektlarini saqlaydi. SYSAUX — uning yordamchisi (qo'shimcha metama'lumot, masalan AWR). Foydalanuvchi ma'lumoti odatda USERS yoki alohida tablespace'da bo'ladi.
explanation.en: The SYSTEM tablespace holds the data dictionary and core system objects. SYSAUX is its auxiliary companion (extra metadata, e.g. AWR). User data normally lives in USERS or a dedicated tablespace.

question: What is the TEMP (temporary) tablespace used for?
options: Temporary work that spills to disk — large sorts, hash joins, and global temporary tables when they don't fit in PGA memory

explanation.uz: TEMP tablespace vaqtinchalik ishlar diskka to'kilganda ishlatiladi: katta ORDER BY/GROUP BY sortlari, hash join'lar va global temporary table'lar PGA'ga sig'masa. Doimiy ma'lumot u yerda saqlanmaydi.
explanation.en: The TEMP tablespace is used when temporary work spills to disk: large ORDER BY/GROUP BY sorts, hash joins, and global temporary tables that don't fit in PGA. No permanent data is stored there.

question: What is a SEGMENT in Oracle's storage hierarchy?
options: The storage allocated to a specific database object (e.g. a table segment, index segment, undo segment), made up of one or more extents

explanation.uz: Segment — muayyan obyektga (jadval, indeks, undo...) ajratilgan xotira. U bir yoki bir nechta extent'dan iborat, extent esa uzluksiz bloklar guruhi. Ierarxiya: Tablespace > Segment > Extent > Block.
explanation.en: A segment is the storage allocated to a specific object (table, index, undo, ...). It consists of one or more extents, and an extent is a set of contiguous blocks. Hierarchy: Tablespace > Segment > Extent > Block.

question: What is the SPFILE (the 'init' parameters)?
options: A data table in the SYSTEM tablespace
The server parameter file (SPFILE, or the older text PFILE init.ora) holding instance startup parameters — SGA sizes, processes, control_files, undo_tablespace, etc.

explanation.uz: SPFILE (yoki eski matnli PFILE init.ora) — instance ishga tushganda o'qiladigan parametrlar fayli: SGA o'lchamlari, processes, control_files, undo_tablespace va boshqalar. SPFILE ikkilik va ALTER SYSTEM bilan o'zgartiriladi.
explanation.en: The SPFILE (or older text PFILE init.ora) is the parameter file read at instance startup: SGA sizes, processes, control_files, undo_tablespace, and more. The SPFILE is binary and changed with ALTER SYSTEM.
